title: DSDS Style Definitions
description: Definitions for documenting visual style guidelines — color palettes, typography systems, spacing scales, elevation levels, and other visual attributes. A style document captures the identity and metadata of a visual attribute domain. Descriptive and presentational metadata (status, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links, etc.) lives in the modular `metadata` array as typed entries. All structured docs live in the unified `documentBlock` array as typed document block objects.

style.description: Documentation for a visual style — the principles, guidelines, scales, and token references that govern a visual attribute such as color, typography, spacing, or elevation across the system. Identity (name, displayName, description) lives at the top level. Descriptive and presentational metadata (status, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links, etc.) lives in the modular `metadata` array — each entry is a typed object with a `kind` discriminator. All structured docs live in the `documentBlock` array — each entry is a typed document block object (principle, scale, guideline, accessibility, etc.).

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a style.

name.description: Machine-readable identifier for the style (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation', 'motion', 'shape'). MUST be unique within the documentation group.

displayName.description: Human-readable name for display in docs (e.g., 'Color', 'Typography', 'Spacing', 'Elevation').

description.description: A description of the style — what visual attribute it governs, how the system approaches it, and its role in the design language. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` discriminator. Types include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this style as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts style-specific types (principles, scale) and general types (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Each document block entry has a `type` property that determines its shape. Ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Express internal artifact relationships (e.g., token group references) through a links metadata entry, not as document block entries.
